name: Apple iPad mini (7th Generation)
introText: The iPad mini 7th generation is the most powerful compact tablet ever made — Apple A17 Pro chip in a 8.3-inch pocket-friendly package for $699 CAD, with Apple Pencil Pro support.
whatWorks: A17 Pro chip is iPhone 15 Pro-class performance in a tablet — handles all apps, games, and workflows without hesitation; future-proofed for many years of iPadOS updates.
8.3-inch size is genuinely pocketable — fits in a jacket pocket or small purse; the only Apple tablet you can take anywhere without a bag.
worthKnowing: "Jelly scroll" effect on the 60Hz display — the LCD display has a slight wobble when scrolling rapidly, a manufacturing characteristic that divided users. Most don't notice in daily use.
No Magic Keyboard compatibility — the iPad mini doesn't support Apple's keyboard/trackpad cases, limiting its productivity use case compared to larger iPads.

commonComplaints.title: Jelly scroll effect
commonComplaints.body: The LCD display scrolls with a slight rolling effect visible when scrolling quickly. Apple acknowledges this as normal behavior; most users adapt or don't notice.

commonComplaints.title: No keyboard case support
commonComplaints.body: The iPad mini doesn't have Magic Keyboard compatibility — it's not designed as a laptop replacement for productivity.

commonComplaints.title: Small size makes typing on-screen harder
commonComplaints.body: The 8.3-inch keyboard is cramped for extended typing. A Bluetooth keyboard is recommended for document work.

verdict: The iPad mini 7 is the best small tablet available — powerful enough to handle any app, small enough to go anywhere. It's the ideal reading device, travel companion, and secondary screen. If you need a keyboard or larger screen, step up to the iPad Air.

realBuyers.quote: Best e-reader that does everything else too. Fits in my jacket. Battery lasts a flight. Perfect travel device.
Use it in the kitchen for recipes. In bed for reading. In the gym for workouts. It goes everywhere.
Gave my kid my old mini, bought the new one. Fastest tablet I've ever used. The A17 Pro is absurd for a mini.

metaDescription: Apple iPad mini 7 review Canada: A17 Pro, 8.3" Liquid Retina, 128GB. $699 CAD. Best compact tablet for Canadian users who want portability and power.
